steps.title: Identify the comparison structure
steps.content: The sentence opens with Compared to that of alumina glass. The pronoun that stands in for a noun, and from context that noun is atomic arrangement. So the phrase means "Compared to the atomic arrangement of alumina glass." The blank must begin with a matching term — the atomic arrangement of silica glass — so the two things being compared are parallel (arrangement to arrangement).

steps.title: Apply the logical/parallel comparison rule
steps.content:
The grammar rule: comparisons must be between like terms. That of alumina glass = the atomic arrangement of alumina glass, so the subject after the comma must be silica glass's atomic arrangement.
  • A: subject is silica glass — compares an arrangement to a substance (illogical comparison)
  • B: subject is silica glass — same illogical comparison
  • C: subject is a significant disadvantage of silica glass — compares an arrangement to a disadvantage (illogical comparison)
  • D: subject is silica glass's atomic arrangement — arrangement to arrangement (parallel and logical)
D is correct.
